notes: Enables >400 MHz DRAM operation; higher bandwidth, lower power than DDR1; not pin-compatible with DDR1. Platforms expected DDR2-based for 2-3 year cycle.
Point-to-point serial I/O replacing parallel PCI. x1 through x16 lane widths. PCIe x4 = 2000 MB/s aggregate; PCIe x16 graphics = ~8 GB/s vs AGP8x ~2 GB/s.
Replaces parallel ATA; thinner cables; interface opens hard-drive I/O bottleneck vs PATA.
Integrated RAID 0 (striping) and RAID 1 (mirroring) with 70-90% read/write speed improvement over single SATA 120GB; standard on all Dell Precision workstations at launch.
x86-64 extensions on Xeon and Pentium 4; 4GB memory ceiling on P4, 16GB on Xeon; uncompromised 32-bit performance; simultaneous 32- & 64-bit application execution.
2-way to 4-way SMP workstations/servers; EM64T at launch.
Single-CPU workstation platform; EM64T at launch with 925X chipset.
New PCIe x16 OpenGL graphics cards targeted at CAD, DCC, Oil & Gas workstation-class applications.
Highly expandable dual-CPU workstation; announced June 28, 2004.
Predecessor of PCIe x16; ~2 GB/s peak; being displaced at this transition.
Parallel SCSI disk interface cited as bottlenecked on shared PCI bus.
375 MB/s per 4-channel SAS adapter; emerging in 2004.
